---
title: "Module 4: Advanced Topics"
---

## Module Overview

This module covers advanced Python topics including file operations, error handling, and working with external libraries and modules.

## Learning Objectives

By the end of this module, you will be able to:

- Read from and write to files
- Work with different file formats (text, CSV, JSON)
- Handle errors and exceptions gracefully
- Create custom exceptions
- Import and use built-in and external modules
- Organize code into modules and packages
- Use popular Python libraries

## Module Contents

### Lesson 1: File I/O
Learn how to read, write, and manipulate files in Python.

### Lesson 2: Error Handling
Master exception handling to create robust programs.

### Lesson 3: Libraries and Modules
Explore Python's module system and popular libraries.

## Prerequisites

You should have completed Modules 1-3 and be comfortable with:
- Variables and data types
- Data structures
- Control flow (conditionals and loops)
- Functions

::: {.callout-tip}
## Why These Topics Matter

These advanced topics are essential for:
- Processing data files for analysis
- Building robust applications
- Leveraging Python's vast ecosystem
- Writing professional-quality code
:::

## Estimated Time

This module should take approximately 6-8 hours to complete, including exercises and practice time.

## Ready to Start?

Begin with [Lesson 1: File I/O](lesson1.qmd)!
